ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Kutaa wúntì ŋwìiń nyáń ní Jaakɔb yíkì Peeniyeel. U-kpìiŋŋu puee, u nìn dúlifí ní.
translated: That night Jacob was left alone at Peniel. A man wrestled with him until dawn.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb lēē binib ki tú u-maan Eesayuu cee Eedoom satin, Seeyiir tiŋki poon.
translated: This is the story of Jacob and Esau, the sons of Isaac, who was the son of Abraham.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Ní Abimeeleeki yíiń Abraam ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba ŋá ní a ŋá-ti mimmee? Tilati ní m yīntiǹ-si ní a bàà làá dū man nì m-beelin yab kí kɔ́ǹ ikpitikpaan poon míǹ? A ŋá-mi digaŋgam un kaa máaǹ bí ŋá unilee.»
translated: And Abimelech said to Abraham, "What have you seen, that you have done this thing? What have I done to you, that you have brought on me and on my kingdom a great sin? You have done deeds to me that ought not to be done."

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Maa láá dākafì míǹ m-pɔbilin ki dóò, ní Reebeeka páaǹní ki túń u-baŋki u-gbigbil pu ki dɔ́miń ki sīntì dinyimbɔbilin ki lúń nnyim, ní m mēē-u yii: <M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀.>
translated: When I was in the room, Reebeeka came and knocked on the door, and I opened it for her, and she said to me: "I am tired, help me to lie down."

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Kaayeen cáá unimpu, ní u cáá dipool ki màl̄ Eenɔki. Boonee ní u máń adiin ní an dūu kpántì kitiŋ, ní u dū u-jipɔɔn yindi Eenɔki ki pú-kì.
translated: I am going to the farm, but I don't know the Ewe language. Boonee said that he will come tomorrow, but he didn't tell the Ewe people the truth.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: ní u yíiń dicindin nib, ní ki bí-bi yii: «Dāá kpèèmaan! Bi cáań-ti ueebruja ú nīn cáá-ti ki gbíilíǹ ní! U dɔ́miń kí bàà dòon̄ nì man dee ní m fáá mbiil.
translated: I was sitting at home, when I heard someone say: "Good evening! Come and eat the food I have prepared for you! I have been waiting for you since morning and I am very hungry."

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Abraam yuntiŋu ŋkùm nín nìn nyáń puee, boonee ŋkùm miba nìn gíti nyáń kitiŋ pu. Isaaki nìn cūtì Geeraar tiŋkin ki ká Fiiliistii yab bɔtiu Abimeeleeki.
translated: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there he called on the name of the Lord, the Everlasting God. And Abraham stayed in the land of the Philistines for a long time. Isaac’s servants dug in the valley and discovered a well of fresh water there. But the herders of Gerar quarreled with Isaac’s herders and said, “The water is ours!” So he named the well Esek, because they disputed with him.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: M tīī-ni tin kɔkɔ pée bí ki cáá kufoouee án nīn sá-ni tijin, kii m nín nìn tīī-ni timool tin kɔkɔ lòoǹ ibee puee. M dū kɔkɔ ki tīī-ni án nīn sá ni-jinti.
translated: I have seen that the old ways are not good for us today, and I have also seen that the old ways are not good for our children. I have left the old ways because I have seen that they are not beneficial.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Niinee ní Looti lēē kitiŋ kin dɔ̄ Jɔɔdan buŋŋu watil púee kɔkɔ, ní ki búntì ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú. Bi-yàkatitam nín jítì miŋyee dee.
translated: When Lot left, he looked back at the city he had left behind, and he turned into a pillar of salt. This is a story for all of us.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Soobaal jipɔmbi di sá Alfan nì Manaati nì Eebaal nì Seefoo nì Oonam.
translated: The names of the five are Alfan and Manaati and Eebaal and Seefoo and Oonam.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: An wiiŋŋu ní Noowa nì u-nimpuu nì u-jipɔmbi Seem nì Kaam nì Jaafeeti nì bi-nimpoobi bitee kɔ́ meelan.
translated: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth and the earth was without form and void and darkness was upon the face of the deep.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Boonee ní utɔntɔnlee yōoǹ u-kpiliu gutaamui saalaa ní ki lēē u-kpiliu gaajawanti poon tiwammɔntiil tikpil, ní ki búntì Aram-Naarayiim, Naakɔɔr tiŋkin watil pú.
translated: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers. Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram. Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon. Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse. Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been Uriah's wife. Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa. Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram. Jehoram was the father of Uzziah. Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah. Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amon. Amon was the father of Josiah. Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon. After the exile to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel. Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor. Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Akim. Akim was the father of Eliud. Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, and Mary was the mother of Jesus who is called the Messiah. So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the exile to the Messiah fourteen generations.

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Tí dū-u kí nyāfiǹ Isimaayeel yab kí taa kpɔ̀-u, kun puee u sá ti-nabiki, ní ti sá fatikubaanti.» Ní u-nabiyaamu gbìl̄ ki tīī nì u.
translated: When Ishmael's mother saw that he was about to die, she put him under a tree, and she sat far away from him.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Ní Biila gíti cáá dipool ní ki màl̄ Jaakɔb kibininjabiki kililiitii.
translated: Behold, Bilah conceived and bore Jacob a son.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Unaaciinee kpēē yii waa làá ŋmā kí líí Jaakɔbee ní u fáá u-nantaŋkpal ní u-kpìiŋŋu yɔ̄tì, ama ní u nì Jaakɔb gbíntìi bíi mɔ̀.
translated: God told Jacob to go back to the land where he was born and to his relatives, and that He would be with him.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Niinee ní Unimbɔti bí Noowa yii:
translated: This is the genealogy of Noah's sons:

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Dalbee ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «A nín sá m-niliuee puee, á nīn tūǹ ditundi míǹ faalaa ki tìì-maaa? An puee tùkù-mi kun a là ḿ dāá pà-see.»
translated: Laban said to Jacob, "Should you serve me for nothing, just because you are my relative? Tell me what your wages should be."

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Ama m gbāam̀-si, bi yaa fātìi fōō-si ní a-pɔbil sɔ̄ŋkèe, a mun ń téetí kí ŋá-mi tiniti kí sòoǹ m-bɔti kí tùkù Faaroo kí nyāntí-mi sarka nee poon.
translated: I was going to the farm, but I heard that the elders were holding a meeting at the chief's house, so I turned and went to join them to discuss how to help Faroo and my sister's marriage issue.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb tú bi yíiń u-nimpoobi Raseel nì Leeya bí dāan̄ u-cee kusaaun laakin u-waŋkuti bée.
translated: Jacob was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and the brother of Esau, and he had twelve sons who became the ancestors of the twelve tribes of Israel.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ní u mēē yii: «Ti-Dindaan, m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu, m gbāam̀-si, dàkà m-kpiliu Abraam a-ŋanti kí yàntī ḿ ká kun m nyàab̀ee díǹ.
translated: I said to him: "Lord, I remember Abraham your servant, and I know that you remember Abraham and how he walked before you and how he kept your commandments."

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Ní Jaakɔb jipɔmbee kɔ́ kitiŋin ki fúkúl̀ anikpilee wanti ní ki kóoǹ bi-wanti kɔkɔ, kun puee bi bíil̀ bi-niisal.
translated: Jacob cooked a pot of food and gave it to his children to eat, but they refused to eat it, saying it smelled bad.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was a very old man, and the Lord had blessed him in every way. One day Abraham’s oldest servant, who was in charge of all his affairs, said to him.

ref: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
text: Boonee ní m gíti dámintì yii didikakabaantiil di lòon̄ idifin iluli ní i nyìm̀ ki ŋān̄.
translated: I don't know how to speak this language, but I am trying to learn it little by little every day.

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Looti nìn fàŋkí kí nīn kóò Soowaar tiŋkin, an puee ní u kpáaǹ u-bisalib liitil ki yíkì ki tin kóòń ajoon pu itaŋkpalɔkɔn.
translated: The people of the village of Soowaar were very happy, because their chief had brought them a great blessing and prosperity.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ŋyunti gbantee Madiyan yab bíǹ dūu bàn̄ Eejipiti ní ki mun nìkī Jooseefi ki nyáfiǹ Pootifaar, ujanjakpil un cí Faaroo cindee.
translated: When the Midianite merchants passed by, they drew Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver. And they took Joseph to Egypt. Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him from the Ishmaelites.
